public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as shrine caretaker.
appearance: Ritual garments, symbolic tokens, and carefully maintained devotional detail make the role legible at a glance.
manner: Balances comfort with correction, sounding gentle until rite, taboo, or consequence is threatened. In Sheresh Communes, that reads as someone curious, controlled, and alert to magical contamination, speaking like someone used to careful containment, and the habits of a shrine caretaker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of shrine caretaker in and around Hearthcoil Commune, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Hearthcoil Commune workable through the obligations of shrine caretaker while managing the immediate pressure of coil failure in Sheresh Communes.
hidden_pressure: Must ensure that rite remains trustworthy even when civic pressure tries to convert it into mere administration. Around Hearthcoil Commune, that usually means carrying coil failure, ration conflict, out-migration without letting the role of shrine caretaker visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Any alliance with Meren Rimebridge can expose the party to taboo, doctrine, or institutional expectation centered on Hearthcoil Commune.
Meren Rimebridge needs discreet aid to prevent a ritual lapse, desecration, or public crisis of trust around Hearthcoil Commune.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Ritual dress, offering marks, and devotional cadence make Meren Rimebridge immediately legible as a spiritual intermediary. In Hearthcoil Commune, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Communal Ration Allocators.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Hearthcoil Commune: shrines, cathedrals, funerary halls, or ritual precincts

first_impression: Meren Rimebridge presents as Balances comfort with correction, sounding gentle until rite, taboo, or consequence is threatened. In Sheresh Communes, that reads as someone curious, controlled, and alert to magical contamination, speaking like someone used to careful containment, and the habits of a shrine caretaker still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Shrine Caretaker, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Sheresh Communes.
what_they_refuse: Meren Rimebridge refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Shrine Caretaker too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Meren Rimebridge can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Meren Rimebridge adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Meren Rimebridge is Known locally through day-to-day work as shrine caretaker. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Shrine Caretaker.
